creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
(In reply to Craig Leres from comment #8)

>> "... I don't think I have the appetite to support a riscv32-esp-elf port. I don't have or use this hardware."

Well, that is a pity. The support of ESP32 RISC-V nased chips seems simple and differs only in the configuration file provided for crosstool-ng!

At least, the RSIC-V 32 binaries (and toolchain) compiles with the port for Xtensa provided by you! These days I'll get some ESP32-C6 units (they are supposed to be RISC-V 32 based) and hopefully I will manage to run the recent ESP-IDF v5.3.1 to compile a simple test/example (like blink or so) as proof of concept.

If you don't feel happy with providing support and quality check for RISC-V, you could mark that port as "BEAT" or something? Since the RSIC-V supporting port would rely on the port you maintain would make the thing whole ... wouldn't it?
